ip_outcome_note: Card name 'Excadrill', Metal type, Stage 1 subtype, flavor text 'Known as the Drill King.' National Pokedex no. 530, Unova Subterrene Pokemon. Confirmed.

trivia_bullets.text: "Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph." The 'Drill King' epithet is Excadrill's consistent Pokedex identity; official species classification is 'Subterrene Pokemon.'

Excadrill (no. 530) was one of the most dominant Gen V OU threats. Sand Rush doubles Speed in sandstorm — with Hippowdon or Tyranitar support, Excadrill was banned to Ubers in BW. Mold Breaker is the alternate niche (bypasses Levitate, Sturdy), useful in Ubers against Giratina-O.

Excadrill is Ground/Steel in-game (TCG types as Metal). Evolves from Drilbur (no. 529). Both introduced Generation V (Black & White, 2010, Unova). The two-stage line literalizes drilling at two scales: Drilbur rotates body as compact drill; Excadrill deploys full-body force as the Drill King.
Illustrated by Lee HyunJung, a Korean TCG artist active in the Sword & Shield era.

related_cards.name: Drilbur
related_cards.note: Base-stage evolution pair (no. 114/236), wave 27 E4 mirror complete
